title: Claustodian Symbol Record
description: One trackable Claude Code symbol (a CLI flag, environment variable, command, config key, or internal config flag), along with its lifecycle metadata and provenance.

symbol.description: The exact flag/var/command/key name, e.g. "--safe-mode" or "CLAUDE_CODE_SAFE_MODE".

type.description: The kind of symbol this record describes.

first_seen.description: Claude Code version where this symbol was first observed (not necessarily first existed; see project README). When `first_seen_estimated` is true this is an upper bound, not a confirmed introduction.

removed_in.description: Version where the symbol disappeared, if known; null otherwise.

deprecated_in.description: Version whose official changelog deprecated the symbol. Present only when the symbol has been deprecated; the symbol still exists (status is `deprecated` in snapshots at/after this version, `active` before).

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the symbol.

provenance.description: Where the symbol's existence was established: an official changelog entry, an official docs reference page, or binary/bundle string extraction.

confidence.description: Confidence in the accuracy of this record. "high" when first_seen is anchored (an introducing changelog bullet or an official docs min-version); "medium" when first_seen is an estimate (see first_seen_estimated).

description.description: Plain-language explanation of what the symbol does. Sourced from official docs when available (see description_source); may be empty when no authoritative description exists yet.

description_source.description: Where `description` came from: an official docs page, an introducing changelog bullet, the command/skill registry extracted from the binary, or the symbol's own `claude --help` output. Absent when the description is empty.

first_seen_estimated.description: True when `first_seen` is an upper bound (the symbol was observed by that version but its true introduction is unconfirmed), e.g. a symbol only ever named incidentally in the changelog or documented without a min-version. Corrected by the binary lane. Absent means false.

source_url.description: Link to the changelog entry that introduced this symbol; null for binary-only finds.

category.description: Grouping used both for topic (startup, networking, ui, filesystem, auth, ...) and for ownership/source scoping (third-party, cloud-auth, ci-detect, runtime, terminal, ...).
